  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Final laser alignment was performed after the suction and discharge flanges were bolted up, violating the correct sequence."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Loosen the piping flanges, perform a preliminary alignment, monitor pipe strain during flange bolt-up to ensure it does not exceed limits, and then perform the final shaft alignment."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to API RP 686 Chapter 6, final shaft alignment must be verified before piping is connected, and then monitored/re-checked during and after flange bolt-up to ensure piping strain does not pull the equipment out of alignment. Performing the final alignment only after the flanges are already bolted up without documenting the pipe strain check sequence is a critical sequence violation that can mask severe pipe stress on the pump casings.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 686 Chapter 6 (Recommended Practice for Machinery Installation and Installation Design - Piping Design and Pipe Stress)"
  ]
}
